statement.en: Systematic Review of Criminal Justice Reforms and Pre-Trial Detention Reduction in Uganda: An Analysis of the 2021–2026 Strategic Agenda
statement.summary: This systematic literature review critically analyses the scholarly discourse surrounding Uganda's criminal justice reforms, specifically the strategic agenda to reduce pre-trial detention. The research problem centres on evaluating the implementation and efficacy of these recent reforms, given the persistent and disproportionate use of pre-trial detention, which undermines defendants' rights and burdens the penal system. Employing the PRISMA framework, the methodology involved a rigorous search and synthesis of peer-reviewed articles, government reports, and analyses from Ugandan civil society organisations published up to and including 2023. Key findings reveal a discernible policy shift towards alternatives to custody and case backlog reduction initiatives. However, the analysis identif

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Document is a systematic review (2026-04-25) analyzing implementation of 2021-2026 reforms through 2023 data. Claims are evidence-based but the reform agenda itself remains in active execution phase. Efficacy findings are provisional pending full completion of the strategic period and longer-term outcome measurement. The document's truth-value is contingent on ongoing empirical validation.
